reviews.review_msg: Very caring upbeat prof. Makes sure every student understands and goes into great detail to answer questions. Only problem is his teaching style is a bit disorganized and i didn't know why were learning certain things. The homework was much harder than the exams. Exam were very easy and fair. Similar to the samples he gives

Such a jolly person. My only criticism is that he stutters like crazy in lecture and can often take a while to get to the point, thus why I'm giving 4. The assignments helped teach us almost everything on the exams. Final project was very free in regards to what we wanted to do it on. Super accessible for questions after class. Stress-free course.

Razvan was great in Set Theory! Lectures were a bit heavy but they drew directly from the textbook (free online) so it was easy to review concepts. He was friendly and helpful during office hours. The quiz and exam questions were very similar to the homework, very fair exams. He also clearly cared about the students' course experience.

(For Set Theory). Good, clear lectures with a reasonable pace. Quizzes and exams were all fair. If you did the homework you will easily pass them. Textbook is old and some of the proofs in it skip tons of steps I did not find trivial. Highlight is the tutorials. This semester it was led by a TA who absolutely carried my grade. Very worth attending!
